title: Veterinarian
description:
Responsibilities:

  • Deliver emergency and critical care to small animals and exotic species during scheduled shifts.
  • Attend daily inpatient rounds at the beginning and end of each shift to ensure continuity of care.
  • Provide in-hospital consultations for emergency cases presented during day and night shifts.
  • Conduct thorough physical examinations, diagnostic testing, and advanced medical treatments.
  • Perform emergency surgical procedures and appropriate diagnostic interventions (e.g., TFAST/AFAST, radiography, endoscopy, CT scans, bloodwork).
  • Interpret diagnostic findings to develop and implement effective treatment plans.
  • Stabilize and manage patients with trauma, toxic exposures, and acute medical conditions.
  • Prescribe and administer appropriate pharmaceuticals based on diagnostics and clinical evaluation.
  • Collaborate closely with veterinary technicians, support staff, and specialty teams to deliver high-quality, multidisciplinary care.
  • Communicate effectively and empathetically with pet owners regarding diagnostics, treatment options, prognosis, and home care instructions.
  • Maintain accurate, timely, and comprehensive patient medical records, including clinical notes, diagnoses, and therapeutic plans.
  • Facilitate interdepartmental transfers to relevant specialists and coordinate follow-up care.
  • Refer cases to external specialists when necessary, including timely updates and discharge summaries to referring veterinarians.
  • Manage a fast-paced and high-volume caseload while upholding professional standards under pressure.
  • Ensure compliance with veterinary ethical standards and internal medical protocols.
  • Participate in ongoing professional development and continuing education to stay current with advancements in emergency veterinary care.
  • Provide mentorship and training to junior team members and assist in fostering a collaborative ECC team environment.


Requirements:

  • Minimum of 2 years’ experience in small animal and/or exotic emergency and critical care practice.
  • Possess excellent communication skills, with the ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, multidisciplinary team environment.
  • Certification in Emergency and Critical Care (ECC) is highly desirable and considered an asset.
  • Proficiency or experience in endoscopy and/or gastroscopy is advantageous.
  • Veterinary degree must be recognized by the Animal & Veterinary Service (AVA) in Singapore.


We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers.
